qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SQL Injection via unescaped LIKE wildcards in front-end event search and REST API
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies LIKE wildcard injection through mc_prepare_search_query() in my-calendar-limits.php where esc_sql() fails to escape LIKE wildcards. The advisory identifies IDOR through unauthorized switch_to_blog() with an attacker-controlled site parameter in my-calendar-widgets.php. Although both affect the My Calendar plugin and share the mcjs_action endpoint, the root causes, vulnerable code paths, and security checks missing are fundamentally different: the advisory is about missing authorization on switch_to_blog (CWE-639), while the finding is about inadequate SQL escaping (CWE-89 family).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes an unauthenticated IDOR via switch_to_blog() in the mc_ajax_mcjs_action handler (my-calendar-ajax.php / my-calendar-widgets.php), where an attacker-controlled 'site' parameter is passed to WordPress's switch_to_blog() without authorization. The only qualifying finding (sql-injection-search) describes LIKE wildcard injection through mc_prepare_search_query() in my-calendar-limits.php — a different vulnerable function, different missing security check, and different root cause, despite being in the same plugin.
evidence: Advisory root cause: switch_to_blog() called with attacker-controlled site parameter in my-calendar-widgets.php without authorization check (CWE-639).

sql-injection-search finding root cause: LIKE wildcard injection through esc_sql() in mc_prepare_search_query() (my-calendar-limits.php) — different vulnerable function, different missing check, different CWE.
The qualifying finding does not match the advisory-described IDOR via switch_to_blog.
